title: Vollkornbrot
subtitle: Dense German whole-grain rye loaf leavened with sourdough
excerpt: A dense, moist German rye bread made almost entirely from whole rye flour and leavened with sourdough. Mixed the night before and baked slowly the next day. The crumb is close-grained and sliceable; the flavour is sour, earthy, and deeply satisfying. Seeds are mixed through the dough. Makes one loaf.
sourceNotes: Vollkornbrot (whole grain bread) is a central part of the German baking tradition, documented in German cookery literature from the nineteenth century onward. The USDA public-domain publications on grain fermentation and rye bread chemistry document the high-acid sourdough fermentation required for rye breads, which degrades the starch-binding pentosans in rye and prevents gumminess. The formula — predominantly whole rye flour, long overnight fermentation, loaf tin bake — is consistent with the German Brotordnung (bread order) standards documented in public-domain German food regulation texts.

recipe.freezeNotes: Slice the cooled loaf and freeze in a bag. Slices defrost in 20 minutes at room temperature.
recipe.makeAheadNotes: The flavour deepens significantly on day 2 and 3. Wrap in beeswax cloth and keep at room temperature for up to 5 days.

baking.bakeTemperatureNote: fan oven; the loaf bakes covered for 30 minutes, then uncovered for 35 minutes; the internal temperature should reach 96°C

content.text: Rye dough is not kneadable in the usual sense: rye contains almost no gluten-forming proteins, so the dough behaves more like a thick batter than a bread dough. It is stirred, not stretched. The high-acid sourdough fermentation is essential — it activates enzymes that break down the pentosans in rye, which would otherwise make the crumb gummy. Do not cut the loaf for at least 24 hours after baking; it needs time for the crumb to set.

Combine the {{rye-flour}}, {{strong-bread-flour}}, {{sourdough-starter}}, {{sea-salt-fine}}, and {{water}} in a large bowl. Stir vigorously with a sturdy wooden spoon or spatula until thoroughly combined; it will look and feel like a very thick porridge. Stir in the {{sunflower-seeds}}, {{pumpkin-seeds}}, and {{caraway-seeds}}. Oil a 2 lb loaf tin, pack the dough in firmly with wet hands, and smooth the top. Cover tightly with clingfilm and leave at room temperature for 8 to 12 hours overnight. The surface should look slightly risen and show small cracks or bubbles.

Preheat the oven to 200°C fan. Cover the tin tightly with foil and bake for 30 minutes. Remove the foil and bake for a further 30 to 35 minutes until the loaf has shrunk slightly from the sides, feels firm when pressed, and reaches 96°C internally on a probe thermometer. Turn out onto a rack. Leave to cool completely, then wrap and rest for at least 24 hours before cutting; the crumb sets and the flavour mellows during this rest.
